Caro: Has two mechanics, Embryo Fusion (purple) and Crimson Furnace (yellow). At the end of each turn, you accumulate Embryo Fusion and Crimson Furnace points. Card and equipment (Wheel of Destiny) effects can also contribute to this.

When Embryo Fusion reaches 100%, it resets and puts an Embryo into your hand. You can either use it directly to give an awaker 30 Aliemus and 10% CR, or use a Caro awaker's ultimate (Exalt) to trigger their Devour effect. Devour effects consume Embryos to do additional shit.

Crimson Furnace is just built in healing for Caro realm. You can click on it anytime to heal for that many points. Max Furnace cap and regen is dependent on your max HP.

Aequor: Mechanics revolve around tentacles and tentacle stances. You can switch stances by clicking on the yellow, and the stances available are blue (neutral), white (shield with less damage), or red (more tentacle damage, and tentacles attack each time you deal damage)

You have 1 tentacle by default, additional tentacles will be shown in the red circle. You can click on those tentacles to see your tentacle stats.

Ultra: Mechanics revolve around Ultra Space and Ultra Round. The first card you play in each turn puts a copy of that card into Ultra Space. You can click the void to the left of the Ultra Space to remove the leftmost card which puts an 'Insight' (draw 1 card, gain 1 arithmetica) into your hand 

When the maximum amount of cards have been put into Ultra Space (usually 5), you can enter Ultra Round at the end of your turn. In this round, all the copies in Ultra Space will be put into your hand, and you will get back all of your arithmetica (energy). 

Almost all ultra characters will have a 'Leap' effect on their cards. This basically means that their cards will have additional effects if you play them first.

You should know that you can only mix up to two realms. This means you can do Chaos/Caro or Caro/Aequor, but not Chaos/Caro/Aequor. You get additional stuff if you're using a 'Pure Realm', which basically means you're only using one realm.

If you use a Chaos mix, you get the benefits of a Pure Realm of the other realms. So if you're using Chaos/Caro, you would still get the Pure Realm effects of Caro.

embeds.title: Metagame
embeds.description: A metagame, broadly defined as "a game beyond the game", typically refers to either of two concepts: a game which revolves around a core game; or the strategies and approaches to playing a game. A metagame can serve a broad range of purposes, and may be tied to the way a game relates to various aspe

Personally, I still think it's a yes.

You need meta units like corpo, etc.

Let's take an example: corpo can help you trivialize lots of stages on chapter 1.

Blue realm, by default, is the most OP for chapter 1; they're the least struggling, at least.

But even so, you will see lots of people struggle; even my friends stopped playing at chapters 5-7.

Sure, there are proven F2P units that can clear chapter 1, but it's from "smurf accounts" by veterans experienced with the game.

Newbies don't have this level of knowledge to clear with free units.

My friend left this game before the corpo rerun. If they had corpo and could clear the stages they were struggling with, "maybe," just maybe, they would continue playing.

But their reason for quitting was also from struggling because they "played characters that they liked" with no synergy, and there were no good blue units limited to pull at that time.
